summary: QR Campaign System fully tested. All 19 backend tests passed. All frontend features verified: Campaign CRUD, campaign-specific QR codes, public form with/without campaign badge, admin page with Campaigns/Leads tabs, Campaign and Source columns, filters, Excel export with Campaign column.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/admin/qr-campaigns: Creates campaign with name, generates uppercase alphanumeric code, checks uniqueness (409 on duplicate)
GET /api/admin/qr-campaigns: Lists campaigns with lead_count aggregated from qr_leads collection
DELETE /api/admin/qr-campaigns/{id}: Deletes campaign (leads are NOT deleted)
POST /api/qr-leads with campaign param: Stores lead with campaign_code, campaign_name (resolved), source=CAMPAIGN_QR
POST /api/qr-leads without campaign: Stores lead with campaign_code=null, source=QR
GET /api/admin/qr-leads: Supports campaign_code filter and source_filter (general/campaign)
QRLeadForm.jsx: Shows 'Campaign: {code}' badge when ?campaign= param present, hidden otherwise
AdminQRLeads.jsx: Two tabs - Campaign QR Management (create/view/delete campaigns) and Leads Data (table with filters)
Excel export includes Campaign column with campaign_name || campaign_code || 'GENERAL'
seed_data_creation: Created multiple test campaigns (TEST_CAMP_*, FILTER*, VERIFY*, DUPTEST*, LEADTEST*, UITEST123) and test leads via backend tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: QR Campaign System fully functional. Campaigns: BI6, BI7, UITEST123, and many test campaigns exist. Public form at /qr-lead-form shows campaign badge when ?campaign=CODE param present. Admin page at /admin/qr-leads has two tabs: 'Campaign QR Management' for creating/viewing/deleting campaigns with QR codes, and 'Leads Data' for viewing leads with Campaign and Source columns plus filters. Excel export includes Campaign Name column.

verifications: POST /api/admin/qr-campaigns: Creates campaign with name, generates code
Code is uppercase alphanumeric version of name
Duplicate code returns 409 Conflict
GET /api/admin/qr-campaigns: Lists campaigns with lead_count
DELETE /api/admin/qr-campaigns/{id}: Deletes campaign, returns 404 for nonexistent
POST /api/qr-leads with campaign param stores campaign_code, campaign_name, source=CAMPAIGN_QR
POST /api/qr-leads without campaign stores campaign_code=null, source=QR
Campaign name is resolved from qr_campaigns collection
GET /api/admin/qr-leads?campaign_code=X filters by specific campaign
GET /api/admin/qr-leads?source_filter=general returns only general leads (campaign_code=null)
GET /api/admin/qr-leads?source_filter=campaign returns only campaign leads (campaign_code!=null)
Response includes campaign_codes list for dropdown
/qr-lead-form (no param): Campaign badge NOT shown
/qr-lead-form?campaign=BI6: Campaign badge shows 'Campaign: BI6'
Campaign QR Management tab shows campaign cards
Each card has: code badge, name, description, date, lead count, View QR, Copy URL, Delete buttons
Create Campaign form with name and description inputs
View QR dialog shows QR code with campaign URL, Download and Copy buttons
Leads Data tab shows table with columns: Name, Mobile, City, State, Vehicle Type, Campaign, Source, Date & Time
Campaign column shows campaign_name/code badge or 'GENERAL'
Source column shows 'General' or 'Campaign' badge
Campaign filter dropdown populated from campaign_codes
Source filter dropdown with All/General/Campaign options
Excel export button present
Export includes Campaign column with campaign_name || campaign_code || 'GENERAL'
Export includes Source column
General QR button opens dialog
Shows QR code for /qr-lead-form (no campaign param)
Download and Copy URL buttons present
